items.title: Palestinians react to developments in Gaza ceasefire talks
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Hamas is seeking amendments to the latest U.S. ceasefire proposal for Gaza, a senior official with the group told The Associated Press on Saturday, as Palestinians in the Gaza Strip expressed hope the war would stop soon. Read more: https://bit.ly/4mECxD2 

#gaza #israel #news

items.title: Scene in Paris as PSG scores vs. Inter Milan in Champions League final
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Police were deployed near Parc des Princes stadium in Paris on Saturday to control supporters of Paris Saint-Germain. They let off fireworks and flares as they celebrated their team taking the lead against Inter Milan in the Champions League final. (AP Video/Philippe Marion).

items.title: Monsoon floods in India kill at least 22 people
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Landslides and flash flooding triggered by days of torrential monsoon rains in India’s northeast have killed at least 22 people, officials said Saturday. Five people, including three from a single family, were killed on Saturday when their homes were buried in a mudslide in Assam state's capital city of Guwahati. Authorities in the state disconnected electricity in several areas to reduce the risk of electrocution. Read more: https://bit.ly/451Wf5t 

#india #flood 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Synagogues and Holocaust memorial splattered with paint in Paris
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Five Jewish community sites in Paris were vandalised with green paint overnight from Friday to Saturday, the Paris prosecutor’s office confirmed to French media on Saturday. Authorities have opened an investigation into religiously motivated vandalism.

#paris #news

items.title: How federal public health cuts are affecting communities across the US
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Trump administration is cutting health spending on an unprecedented scale, pulling $11 billion of direct federal support and laying off tens of thousands of workers at national health agencies that support local public health work. It wants billions more slashed. (AP Video: Mary Conlon). Read more: https://bit.ly/4kl5N07 

#health #news 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Throngs of people flocked to southern Hong Kong on Saturday to watch the iconic dragon boat races, a highlight of a Chinese festival with over 2,000 years of history.

items.title: Gaza music teacher helps displaced children learn to play instruments
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Music teacher Ahmed Abu Amsha and his family now call a tent in the once vibrant Al-Jundi Al-Majhool Square in Gaza City, home. Forced into the makeshift displacement camp, Ahmed is trying to turn despair into hope by teaching music to displaced children. 

#gaza #music
